metadata.purpose: Synthetic ICU cases for UI testing, agent orchestration, risk scoring, and retrieval demos
metadata.schema: PatientData-compatible (id, name, age, sex, weight, daysInICU, admitDiag, vitals, labs, medications, notes)
metadata.generation_note: All records are synthetic and deidentified. Values are ICU-like, clinically plausible, and designed for agent testing rather than research use.

notes.author: Dr. S. Sharma (Attending)
notes.text: Patient with chills, tachycardia, fever, and borderline blood pressure. Empiric broad-spectrum antibiotics started. Blood cultures drawn.

notes.author: Nurse P. Okafor (Day Shift)
notes.text: Patient increasingly confused. GCS down to 13. BP trending lower. Fluids increased and attending notified.

notes.author: Dr. R. Martinez (Evening)
notes.text: Persistent fever and rising WBC. Lactate trending up. Oxygen requirement increased. ICU review requested.

notes.author: Night Nurse L. Chen
notes.text: Norepinephrine started overnight. Urine output low. Creatinine returned markedly elevated versus prior. Repeat labs ordered.

patients.name: Ms. Maria Rodriguez
patients.admitDiag: Post-op Day 2 - Bowel Resection - ARDS Developing

notes.author: Dr. A. Patel (Surgical ICU)
notes.text: Post bowel resection, hemostasis adequate. Patient extubated in OR. Some abdominal distension noted.

notes.author: Dr. A. Patel
notes.text: Oxygen requirement increasing overnight. Bilateral crackles present. CXR shows bilateral infiltrates - ARDS pattern suspected.

notes.author: Nurse ICU B. Osei
notes.text: Re-intubated at 02:30. FiO2 60 percent, PEEP 8. Urine output decreasing. Bilirubin up. Patient sedated and not following commands.

notes.author: Dr. B. Singh (Nephrology)
notes.text: AKI after contrast CT. Creatinine peaked at 2.8. Baseline around 1.0. Holding nephrotoxins. Hydration started.

notes.author: Dr. B. Singh
notes.text: Creatinine trending down. Urine output recovering. Good response to fluids. Continue monitoring.

notes.author: Nurse Day Shift
notes.text: Patient alert, mobilising, and tolerating oral intake. O2 not required. Potential discharge to ward in 24-48 hours if trend continues.

notes.author: Dr. S. Sharma (Attending)
notes.text: Fever, tachycardia, and worsening hypotension. Broad-spectrum antibiotics started. Blood cultures obtained. Case trajectory currently improving. Septic Shock - Source Control Pending.

notes.author: Respiratory Therapy
notes.text: Mentation is worse. Perfusion remains borderline despite fluids. Repeat lactate and cultures pending. Name in record: Ms. Elena Diallo.

notes.author: Dr. A. Patel (Surgical ICU)
notes.text: Pressor support required. Urine output falling. Repeat labs show rising inflammatory markers. Continue ICU monitoring and reassess at next lab cycle.
